mnemonics.title: SS Partitioning: 'A Boy Asked Everything'
mnemonics.content: Remember SST = SSA + SSB + SSAB + SSE as 'A Boy Asked Everything about the Total.' A = Factor A, B = Factor B, AB = their interaction, E = Error. These four components explain the Total variation in a two-way ANOVA with replication.

mnemonics.title: Three F-tests: 'Always Be Interacting'
mnemonics.content: In two-way ANOVA with replication, you perform three F-tests: F_A (Always test Factor A), F_B (Be sure to test Factor B), F_AB (test the Interaction). All three use MSE in the denominator. Remember: 'Always Be Interacting with Error' -- all F-tests divide by MSE.

mnemonics.title: No Replication, No Interaction
mnemonics.content: When n = 1 (no replication), think 'No Repetitions, No Interaction test.' Without multiple observations per cell, you cannot separate interaction from error. The design becomes SST = SSA + SSB + SSE only, and the error term absorbs any interaction effect.
